title: Notebook code-cell replace does not reset execution_count or outputs
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / State Management (Tell, Don't Ask)

## Location
src/tools/file/notebook.rs:165-187 (replace branch of `execute_notebook_edit`)

## Violation
On a code-cell replace, only `source` and optionally `cell_type` are updated:

```rust
cells[index]["source"] = source_to_line_array(new_source);
if let Some(ct) = cell_type {
    cells[index]["cell_type"] = json!(ct);
}
```

The stale `outputs` array and `execution_count` from the previous source remain attached to the new source. Re-saving a notebook with `print('hello')` as source but `outputs: [{"text": ["world\n"]}]` produces a corrupted notebook where output is from code that no longer exists. The test `notebook_replace_code_cell_does_not_reset_outputs` (line 524) explicitly pins this as a current bug.

## Mandated refactor
In the replace branch, after assigning `source` (and when the resolved cell is type code, or when `cell_type` is being changed to code), set `cells[index]["outputs"] = json!([])` and `cells[index]["execution_count"] = Value::Null`. Add a regression test that asserts both fields are reset after a code-cell replace.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
